channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 134 replies — 536.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 134 • Updated: 2026-06-06 06:03 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62319609
>>62313866
>cll makes hundreds of millions of $ worth of money through the scale program, and this shit will continue increasing, the DTCC or swift also have private contracts through the company called SmartContract.com
wait is this true? Can I verify this somewhere?

62319647
>>62314036
this makes no sense though, if they buyback to refill the reserve it will be buy pressure on the markets contrary to before the premine has run out.

62319800
>>62319507
amen but fuck wage slaving, i literally stopped waging, i just ride bullruns, and then wait 4 years until sergey finally baby carries me to the land of "make it" even though i consider myself blessed alreafy because i didnt wage for the last 2 years thx to sergey.

fuck all the degen retards who ape into shitcoins i hope they all burn and fuck off, meanwhile us linkies are guaranteed to stay until retirement because chainlink has nowhere to go but become huge and central to all of the future of finance ammennn

>>62319609
go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees

>>62319647
why not? thet are right now using the preminted tokens to pay for expenses and subsidizing all while having a reserve, bas8cally the exceess money is put into a dca into the reserve so they can continue this game forever, also nobody said theyd only buy 1m every week forever, i hope they increase it

62319862
>>62312685
I don’t think there’s much OTC Link to be bought. In any case even OTC buys support price over time. And you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.

62319875
>>62312881
I think it’s still a pretty important token in the crypto space, but that doesn’t necessarily make the token valuable. That’s why it causes so much heartache here.

62319882
>>62319800
>go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees
obviously I'm not asking to verify the fact that there are 9 employees you absolute retard

62319898
>>62313883
>he forgot about postmine

62319917
>>62319862
>you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.
It's extremely possible. Link has millions in volume every day, people could buy ungodly amounts in a matter of weeks. And as the price tries to go up, Bitcoin simply dumps and dumps and dumps again to keep it low. Like when it kept Link under $9 for over a year.

62319918
>>62319882
i ncan't fucking post links on 4chan you fucking mongoloid
but the company does exist and it's the one having private deals with swift
>rocketreach*dot*co*slash*smartcontract-profile_b54a3c45f6db61a8

62319921
>>62319875
Meanwhile over half the coins with higher market caps are literally useless yet more valuable.

62322856
>>62318952
>>>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
>How is this different from Sergey selling?
>Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

https://etherscan.io/address/0x9b1c1752b6b6eaf62a8c491a8681b32c1f7ecde9#tokentxns

Can you prove that all the link tokens Chainlink Labs moved from their noncirculating token wallets to Binance went straight to the node operators? If you can't prove it, your analogy falls apart.

62323005
>>62316188
>Why did XLM pump +80% on lesser DTCC news then?

i don't know dude, maybe bc their liquidity is pretty thin

how many times do i have to fucking tell you people that the market isn't fair

you are essentially complaining that your scam isn't working as good as it used to. you realize that right?

>>62316251
>Yes anon, Bitcoin dumping every time Link has big news is my fault.

you are proving my point
if you're convinced there's a conspiracy to stop your gains, why would you continue holding
you are basically saying you know for sure it's impossible to make money, and yet you continue the same path

einstein would refer to that as insanity

62323033
>>62316263
>XLM is still higher than before the pump started, LINK prints new monthly lows
>if what the shill claimed was true, LINK would exhibit similar performance


the kid you are arguing against actually thinks he can debate his way into changing reality

i don't know if it's a combination of autism, naivety, or he's just plain retarded, but that's his thought process. he literally believes if he can show people how the market is "wronging" him, it will change things

62323065
>>62316401
>I bought in prior to mainnet so I am well into the green

fact check: you're down 80% and desperate

>and am living off my staking rewards.

LOL

fact check: you have enough money to survive 5-10 years and then will move back home if you have even left yet

>I sympathize with you anon since if this were XRP,

fact check: it's not xrp

> You're doomed to wait till after the Clarity Act

fact check: clarity act will have zero upward pressure on link price

>when banks finally get their markets up and running

what the fuck does that even mean. banks run fine every single day

>Real World Assets NFT treading

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHA

62323184
>>62316444
>Chainlink network must use the LINK token to secure the network

fact check: chainlink network currently operates fine without link token. (offshore labs company makes offchain deals and full staking version with slashing not released nor coming any time soon)

>>62316605
>. This program is a way to grow these companies, the actual users of the network and get them up and running and profitable which would drive more usage of the Chainlink network which should drive the value up.

fact check: these companies are not growing. they are grifts with no users, no profit, and have no value accrual to the link token

>>62316776
>duuuude chicken and eggs

that's hilarious zach still posts here. you belong in jail

62323264
>>62317156
>oh cool adem's here. serious question why do you live on this board 24/7?

don't project you little fat skinny weirdo

62323308
>>62317182
>you're very smart, because while i read you i have thoughts that come up, and you answer those thoughts. bene lurking 4chan since 2007 or some shit i've learnt over the years to spot smart / legit people, and you are one, it's rare to meet good people


fact check: wow this is getting pretty gay

>>62317182
>they keep fudding for absolutely no reason for all these years

what exactly do you mean by the term "fud"
are you talking about saying what the literal price is, or what's actually going on? they call that reality

>>62317656
>duuuude the world went to shit after covid
>To bring in this new system that Link facilitates. And we get to be on top while the trash get fucked. It’ll be a lot more meaningful cuz we will be able to dab.

HAHAHAHAAHAHAA

62323333
So many deranged cuckolds are going to HODL this shitcoin until its back to $2

62323719
when does the humiliation end, stinkies
when do you finally rise up and say, NO

62324418
>>62323333
checked
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62324495
I'm new to this whole "investing" thing but isn't holding an asset that's down 70% over 5 years, like, a really bad investment? Or is it good that it goes down because you can make more money losing money like those Hollywood studios do? Just seems weird to still like and hold a thing so much that seemingly only brings you pain and suffering.

62324507
>>62314036
Why would CLL want the token to be dirt cheap when they are the primary holders and benefactors. Why a slow bleed when they could have a massive run up and dump eventually?

62324652
>>62324507
they don't need the token to appreciate in value, why? because the private deals they make bring em steady revenu that is independent of the link price, because they subsidize the network using link tokens, that are preminted, lets say to run the network for a wholz ass year it costs 50k link in fees, they get paid their 300M$ of scale money no matter how much the network fees are, they dont care they dont buy the link, in essence they are basically indirectly selling their link for cash through private deals and way higher than market price

62324749
>this board is still composed of linkie cuckolds in the big 2026.
Why?

62324777
>>62324495
generally speaking, yes. But you don't go by rule of thumbs if you're looking for those once in a lifetime golden egg investments. Chainlink is a special case in that they're the only crypto company banks are integrating their technologies with.

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62324797
>>62324749
Why? Because 4chan is a safe place for all kinds of losers. (I love you all)

62324959
>>62323184
lol if I was CLG I wouldn't have to post here I'd be retired because that faggot grifted his way to where he is. The irony though of you always assuming everyone is your twitter e-celebs (the verbose xrp shill does the same with fishy iirc), meanwhile you literally post here every day, same prose, same twitter screencaps, often quoting your own twitter account. Its just bizarre.

62325222
>>62324777
checked! thanks for posting the dtcc link.
https://x.com/TomZschach/status/2062907080931946674
https://x.com/SmartContract/status/2062670460412625036
big banks including jp morgan announced they want to launch a tokenized deposit network next year also.
Means DTCC which settles stocks AND the big banks need Chainlink

62325301
>>62325222
>>62324777
nice, very nice
But yes, while you can point to 5 years down 70% and be pissy, you can also say, for something with solid fundamentals, its the perfect time to buy. All a question of if crypto is legitimate or remains a scam casino.

62327001
>>62322856
I don't have to prove that at all, you specifically said the mechanism whereby token from the uncirculating supply are used to pay the nodes "decouples the link price from the usage of the network".

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-06 06:04 UTC

OP

This is the lowest BTC will ever be

62326802
>>62326790
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOO WAGMI MUMUBROS

62326808
>>62326790
>>62326802
CHECK THE FUCKING CHART

62326811
>>62326808
we're at the bottom retard. bitcoin will never reach $55k for the rest of humanity.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326812
>>62326790

62326815
>>62326811

62326816
>>62326811
I'm so glad you said this

62326992
Just buy the dip people. Roast a bear today

62326998
>>62326790
>This is the lowest BTC will ever be
delusion

62327000
>>62326998
It'll never not be funny that thr black dude who played barney wrote sex domination books

62327003
Bobos tried so hard but after that rejection its over

62327004
>>62327003
Its just a little free bleed anon... I got a heavy cycle coming

item.title: How do you cope with the fact that bears WILL FUD during the picobottom? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you cope with the fact that bears WILL FUD during the picobottom?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-06 05:59 UTC

OP

How do you cope with the fact that bears WILL FUD during the picobottom? Im trying to find an entry price, but I cannot force myself to click the buy buttom because it always feels like it's going lower. But then you look back and the same confident bearposts look like memes.

Look at this fucking picture. You are still up 700% since this retard convinced me to not buy MSTR at the bottom. At the very fucking BOTTOM. Read this again. You are up 700% while mongs talk about muh Saylor liquidation is going to kill BTC or whatever the fuck latest FUD narrative is.

And now he's back again all over X in X Spaces trying to convince me that's all going to crash without no brakes. Fucking mindfucking niggers. It's been 17 years of this shit and Bitcoin always manages to make yourself look retarded by not buying.

I keep trying to find arguments about how this time is different so I don't buy but it's all the same dogshit again:

>muh chinaban
>muh forks
>muh quantum
>muh old coin
>muh saylor
>muh AI

There's always some nigger argument about not buying to make you feel comfortable and then you look back and want to punch a bear.

Yet, the main thesis remains: Governments are FORCED to print money and buy back their own debt when yields go up above a certain treshhold that keeps going lower over time (currently 5% on the 10Y is already making the FED nervous, remember it used to be much higher). Any other asset you buy gets tampered with as it's all on the accounting inside the system iself that allows for such fuckery. But Bitcoin has no value.

Early replies (first 3)

62324468
This is so fucking retarded. We have this decentralized immutable ledger network that will never be replicated again, but people are putting their surplus capital on AI slop CAPEX as the last shinny object. Not because the CAPEX makes any fundamental sense at all, but due financial speculation and fear of not beating inflation enough.

You just KNOW the Bitcoin thesis plays out long term, and you know bears just want to buy lower, but at the same time you have this constant fear of it going lower ("there is always a better entry point") and you never buy. I have been watching the price since 2013 and never bought because of that. It's a brutal asset.

62324494
>>62324458
>>62324468
your charts seem a little, uh, 'outdated' here chief.
gad to help tho.

62324506
>>62324494
You seem a little uh, retarded chief. Check again and learn about stock splits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62324578
so, MSTR stopped trading in 2025, when your charts, uh, stop? Even tho its last split was in 2024?
what is this Piece of Shit that is down 36% this month then?
Have the SEC been round yet btw?
I'm sure they know the way already.

62324682
>>62324578
Holy retard.

62324729
>>62324458
You buy when it hits a price you would have killed to get yesterday/last month/whatever. That's how you buy picobottoms. There will always be FUD, you won't get good prices without FUD.

62324825
>>62324458
ya, this is the shitty part about getting started in the stock markets at a later age, you really do have to live through 1-2 bottoms before you realize that the bear posts show up at every fkin bottom on the way down and on the way up

these people are fucking retards, and only marginally more retarded than the bulls that shill at every fucking top on the way down and on the way up. The only reason they're more retarded is because the money printer bails out the retardation of the bull shills

just gotta live through it is my advice

it's kinda like what a dad would say about to a kid who is afraid of the pain of failure. If you don't experience failure, how will you learn to be resilient in life? You don't, so the failure is actually a good thing and the pain not to be avoided

62326900
Im also kind of scared of complete missing the bottom as this is my first full cycle I just know I’m gonna fuck it up somehow

62326937
I started DCAing a couple months ago at the last bottom. You'll never time the bottom perfectly just like you'll never time the top perfectly. Idk where we will go but bitcoin will certainly be above these prices one day again.

62326990
>>62324825
The bulls point out the money printer is going to bail them out so are they actually that retarded?

ADEM WON!!! HE FVCKING WON!!! HE HAD GODLY POST NUT CLARITY FROM COOMING! HE HAD VISIONS NONE OF US COULD SEE AND NONE OF US BELIEVED HIM. ADEM WAS SO FUCKING RIGHT. WE SHOULD HAVE LISTENED AND SOLD LINK AT $30 WHICH IS THE EXACT TOP HE PREDICTED. ADEM WON HOLY SHIT I REALLY AM A SISSY KEKOLD LINK KEK BAGHOLDER. SERGEY IS MY BULL AND IM A FUCKING BAGHOLDING KEK FOR LIFE.

62326951
jeetlinkers going to sub $1

item.title: And just like that BTC is never going below 60k again! — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
And just like that BTC is never going below 60k again!

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-06 05:52 UTC

OP

And just like that BTC is never going below 60k again!

Early replies (first 3)

62326901
>>62326898
it's incredible. that will be the last time in our entire lives that bitcoin will ever be below $60k, historic moment to be honest.

62326915
>>62326901
Truly shocking anyone could pass up this opportunity to build generational wealth without buying.

62326929
>>62326915
it's literally giving up free money, you have to be terminally retarded to not get another reverse mortgage to buy bitcoin on 10x leverage right now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326940
>>62326898
Except the picobottom eom at 55k.

62326942
>>62326929
This, do it on binance gweilo!

62326958
>>62326898
peak last supercycle was 62k so 60k is a safe floor we only can go up from here. great time to come in with large margin positons.

62326964
>>62326898
Official bottom

62326971
>>62326898
Three fucking times they dumped it below and three fucking times it bounced back. If this isn't a bottom signal, idk wtf would be.

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 74 replies — 30.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-06-06 03:40 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62315335
How rekt in anus is OP right about now?

62315341
>>62309503
I am gonna put my money where my mouth is as buy 15k in BTC at current price and post proof here.

BUYROG is never wrong

62315377
>>62309503
nah, I can't feel the doom, people are expecting sixty-something. It's going to mid 50s

62318708
>>62315194
Are you sure?

62318770
Try $35,000
I'm buying at $38,500

62318780
What about the quantum fud tho?

62318798
>>62309503
I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits. Obvious sell signal. Bottomless shitcoin.

62318815
>>62312131
More like 20k

62319815
>>62318798
>I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits.
LULLLLLLLL

62319876
>>62309503
I'm afraid it will be lower, we're only halfway in the bear market

62320358
>>62309503
>65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

62320385
So far, there is no reason not to believe in the 4 year cycle. And the 4 year cycle tells US you should be buying below the 200 week MA. We just hit It. You should start accumulating right now

62320391
I LOVE those heckin cope threads. Like if some retard making a strong statement on this board is actually going to influence price action. Your brains have been fried by a decade of shitcoining and pump n dumping on other anons. Deal with the real world you absolute goy trash meats. We're going to 2 digits for btc wether u believe it or not.

62320405
>>62309503
BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless. Sheep will figure it out eventually, until then they follow the pedo coin BTC congrats

62320466
>>62320405
>BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless!!111111111
>my XYZ coin (0.0003 BTC) is real bitcoin!!!!!111111

62320468
>>62309503
The city of OP

62320500
>>62320466
>inb4 BCH is not Bitcoin either, only BSV is

62320588
>>62320500
only Bit-Diamond XD
at least they changed hash afair to not compete for same ASICs (with predictable result)

62320857
>>62320358
Good. At least now the dipshits will sell the bottom.

If you think you are getting the upperhand, you are not.

It is better for BTC to get rid of the financially illiterate than it to be worth 200k but held by imbeciles trying to scam each other.

Bitcoin is a storage of value. Want proof? Zoom out the chart.

We are winning the fight against bullshit Fiat currency, dwarven suckers, and boomer indexes.

Everyone talking shit here WISHES they had bought in years ago. Sour grapes everywhere

The BUYROG remains.

>>62320391
>cope thread

I made free money to anyone who wasn't dumb enough to now sell the bottom and make this a sucker's rally.

>>62320405
Was waiting for the inevitable BTC will go to zero comment.

It is the bottom signal.

62320897
>>62309535
>I don't need proof
>BTC 5k below your prediction.

Faggot.

62320920
>>62309503
OHNONONONONONONONONONONONONO LMAOOO

62323245
Any last words, OP?

62323584
>>62323245
>Any last words, OP?

62323600
>>62323584
>OP rn

62323880
>>62323245
>>62323584
>>62323600
YOU LOST, TRANNY

It doesn't matter if Bitcoin falls to 59k, I repeat what I said: it is good riddance that the yuppies go find another con to shove down people's throats because they are overleveraged and undereducated. No one wants their store value reserve being speculated on by retards who laugh as they sell the bottom, then complain they can't make money.

Stupidity is a tax on success.

Also, keep in mind, BTC might be 6% loss in a day, SILVER and GOLD are doing WORSE. You can't compare BTC performance against stock because it is not apples to apples, BTC can only be compared with other crypto in its asset class, and other storage value investment like PMs. And guess what? It still blows all of them out of the water, and the trade volume of BTC evaluated in any desk is far greater than all of crypto and rock collections combined. BTC brockerage platforms exist by the thousands, how many dealers of PMs exist?

I will put this even simpler for bear trolls: read the Bible, flip it open to the parable of the talents, and read what is said about the one faggot who thought he was smug and clever because he neither won, nor lost the money loaned to him. This is you, this is the whole bear market.

I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes. I'm going laughing my way to the bank enjoying this fire sale of BTC owned by the eternal underclass of statist peasants.

BTC will be 70k by the end of the month. Hell, it will be 60k+ by the end of the day.

If I called the bottom 10% above, it is because I overestimated the intelligence of the morons that do me a favor by forgetting this asset class exists.

May they buy ETFs and go hold the bag of billionaire scammers.

I repeat:
>if you bought, hold
>if you haven't, enjoy the sale
>if you sold, we are better without you

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

62326531
The falling knife is around $32.8k

Screencap this and thank me later.

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

item.title: WE'RE GOING DOWN DOWN IN AN EARLIER ROUND — 7 replies — 24.0/hr

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62326705
as long as it doesn't go below 53k (last bottom), there is nothing to worry about. it's just a fucking nothingburger honestly.

62326710
>>62326705
LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62326712
>>62326700
Your ass goes down in the 6th. Say it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326713
>>62326710
it's the truth, this whole thing is fucking free money so far. 53k hard diamond cock support that is undefeatable. bitcoin will never in history go below 53k. screencap this if you want you fucking retard.

62326882
>>62326721
saved. cant wait to post this in a month to laugh at your face when we go back to $70k

62326963
>>62326700
I COOOOOOOOMED SO HARD BY MY PUTS HAVING GONE WILD!

item.title: Now my advice: HOLD — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Now my advice: HOLD

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-06 06:06 UTC

OP

BTW, $ETH is trading below 2018 price, adjusted for inflation.
Since you all need to learn a lesson today: Take life easy, but be serious in investment. Diversify. Buy the dips in indices. Not all in on a single industry like crypto.

Early replies (first 3)

62324738
>>62324681
I'm not buying your bags boomer

62324790
>>62324681
No thanks, I'm all in on BTC

62326993
>>62324790
lel

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326997
>>62324681
>BTW, $ETH is trading below 2018 price, adjusted for inflation.
man this is grim if true.

62327006
>>62326993

item.title: ding ding ding, the serious thread — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ding ding ding, the serious thread

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-06 06:01 UTC

OP

Memes aside, what causes this deep in Bitcoin demand currently.

Is it rising price of power? Along with better speculation on oil, and DDR (shares and so on)?

How low will it go, near 50k? 40k? And why.
When do we expect next real bullrun - above 100k?

It was fun to doompost, but now we need to decide how much to slurp and at what levels. And how much to borrow/obtain for it.

Early replies (first 3)

62326357
>>62326341
It's the great shakeout (Of institutions).

Slurp the absolute bottom.

Jamie Dimon didn't kill himself.

62326526
>>62326341
Causes: Saylor doing a micro-dump, Iran war, semiconductors sector down, AI-centric security fears (see Zcash).

Current possible bottom in current conditions: around 50k at worst.

Possible bottom if economic and financial situation worsens considerably (see Iran war and its consequences on Western supply chains, credit tightening, stock markets dropping, Saylor having to sell massively…): well below 10k.

Next bull run: depends on how low it will start, and on the upcoming monetary policy, but I believe the bull run will be extremely dramatic if we’re starting from a low point. BTC at $1,000,000 sounds like a joke today, but back then in 2014, hoping that BTC would reach $10,000 sounded equally delusional and absolutely out of reach. It was even a running gag in the chat rooms ("BTC $10,000 SOON" when everyone was desperate because it was stuck at $250). And yet…

Never forget BTC has the potential to overtake physical gold, whose "market cap" is almost 30 times higher than BTC so far. So I expect up to $1,000,000 from the next bull run, if the circumstances are favorable. But $300,000 would be cool enough.

62326848
>>62326526
>Current possible bottom in current conditions: around 50k at worst.
>Source: I made it up.

FUCKING ZERO.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326864
>>62326848
Bobo's get greedy too.

62326996
>>62326864
Since I am white, I don't have a single dime in anything crypto related.

62324960

way more pain in his voice

CUZ I'M GOING DOWN IN SATS ON A RED LINE

item.title: USDT flips ETH — 11 replies — 3.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
USDT flips ETH

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-06 03:07 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62323404
>>62323391
at this rate its gonna flip BTC

62323434
Crypto is healing. The only legit use case for crypto are stablecoins.
I hope we can have a top 10 with only stablecoins in the coming years.

62323466
>>62323404
until someone remembers, 15%+ of this trash's 'reserve' is worthless BTC.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62323883
>>62323434
>The only legit use case for crypto are stablecoins.
I feel like that should be more obvious. Its cryptocurrency, its meant to be used to pay for things, not a speculative asset.

62323928
>>62323391
ze actual flippening kek

62323962
3 digit shitcoin eoy

62324025
Tether bros! Are we breaking out of the multi-year $1 resistance?

62324113
>>62323883
Its quite interesting. cointards dont seem to get it but the *point* of ethereum requires that the value of the coin be low. ethereum working as intended means that coins are exchanging hands at the monetary rate required to pay for the compute to run the network + a small %. I do not understand why anybody would expect the price of ethereum to go up. it is antithetical to the purpose of the machine.

This is all a good thing by the way. This is the pathway to actual intrinsic coin value. The value of the ethereum coin being tied to actual meaningful data processing work, that is.

62326419
>>62323962
which one

62326430
Don't a lot of people from sanctioned countries use USDT?

62326441
>>62326430
Yes, and they got fucked recently. Shitcoins are not a secure, private store of wealth and they never will be. It's another way to control the goyim with fiat magic numbers.

item.title: Is microstrategy a good stock? — 5 replies — 1.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is microstrategy a good stock?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-06 03:11 UTC

OP

Should I ignore the FUD and invest?

Early replies (first 3)

62325143
>>62325135
no
/thread

62325190
>>62325135
well, they haven't been CAUGHT committing accountancy fraud for a bit tbf. But as currently $12B-odd underwater, perhaps not the best bet for the pension.

62325205
>>62325135
Hey guys I read rat poison is bad for you but should I eat it?
>Exactly what this thread sounds like

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326343
>>62325135
It's like BTC except more volatile. When BTC spikes MSTR spikes more. When BTC tanks MSTR tanks more. Not for me, but if you feel like trying to time things I think it's not bad.

62326450
>>62326343
>It's like BTC
its toilet paper ran by a known scammer with a proven penchant for cooking the books. And that under the exact same fucking ticker. Theres also the small matter of him being $12B odd in the hole.
>its volatile
yeah, no shit it is. Now given none of this is, in any way, arguable either, why in Christs name would anyone touch this hot garbage over BTC? Literal lunacy.
